perturbation.original_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client. Chemsultants hereby agrees it shall be responsible for any breach by it or its Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Representative” shall mean the party’s affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants) and controlling persons, who are actively and directly participating in any transaction with Client or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of furthering any transaction with Client, and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential, except when disclosure is required for routine internal business processes; and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client. Chemsultants hereby agrees it shall be responsible for any breach by it or its Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Representative” shall mean the party’s affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants) and controlling persons, who are actively and directly participating in any transaction with Client or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of furthering any transaction with Client, and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction within Section 2. It states that Chemsultants agrees to keep Confidential Information confidential, but then adds 'except when disclosure is required for routine internal business processes'. This creates ambiguity regarding what constitutes 'routine internal business processes' and whether that overrides the confidentiality obligations, causing a direct conflict with the initial commitment to confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential, except when disclosure is required for routine internal business processes; and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client. Chemsultants hereby agrees it shall be responsible for any breach by it or its Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Representative” shall mean the party’s affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants) and controlling persons, who are actively and directly participating in any transaction with Client or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of furthering any transaction with Client, and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the confidentiality agreement ('except when disclosure is required for routine internal business processes'), which directly contradicts the original commitment to keep all Confidential Information confidential as stated in Section 2. This creates an ambiguity regarding what constitutes 'routine internal business processes,' making the confidentiality obligation unclear and potentially unenforceable. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Documents. Upon written request by Client at any time, Chemsultants shall promptly return all Confidential Information, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in the party’s p o s s e s s i o n o r i n t h e p o s s e s s i o n o f a n y R e p r e s e n t a t i v e o f t h e p a r t y , p r o v i d e d , however, Chemsultants shall be permitted to charge Client for all reasonable costs incurred by Chemsultants for compliance with this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Documents. Upon written request by Client at any time, Chemsultants shall promptly return all Confidential Information, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in the party’s p o s s e s s i o n o r i n t h e p o s s e s s i o n o f a n y R e p r e s e n t a t i v e o f t h e p a r t y , p r o v i d e d , however, Chemsultants shall be permitted to retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, and Chemsultants shall be permitted to charge Client for all reasonable costs incurred by Chemsultants for compliance with this Section.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 5 allows Chemsultants to 'retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes'. This contradicts the primary purpose of the NDA, which is to ensure that Confidential Information is kept confidential and not retained after the termination or request of the Client. This directly contradicts Section 2, which mandates keeping all information confidential. The allowance of keeping a copy creates an inherent contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential, except when disclosure is required for routine internal business processes; and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client. Chemsultants hereby agrees it shall be responsible for any breach by it or its Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Representative” shall mean the party’s affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants) and controlling persons, who are actively and directly participating in any transaction with Client or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of furthering any transaction with Client, and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 allows Chemsultants to retain a copy of the confidential information for archival purposes. This directly contradicts Section 2, which states that Chemsultants must 'keep all Confidential Information confidential' and 'not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit'. By retaining a copy, Chemsultants is inherently using and possessing confidential information, which is a breach of the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 2. The original text did not allow for any retention of documents, but required them to be returned at the Client's request.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages would be an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the non-breaching party shall be entitled to injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages may be considered an adequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the non-breaching party may seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that money damages would be an insufficient remedy. However, the modified version introduces uncertainty by stating that money damages 'may be considered an adequate remedy.' This creates a contradiction, because the original agreement specifies that money damages are not sufficient, creating the opposite meaning in Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages may be considered an adequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the non-breaching party may seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text states money damages would be an insufficient remedy, while the changed text states they 'may be considered an adequate remedy.' Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, this change directly creates an in-text contradiction as it introduces ambiguity and conflicts with the initial assertion that money damages were insufficient. This aligns with the provided definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and cannot be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part unless approved in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and can be modified orally, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part if both parties verbally agree on the change and document it via email.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement stated that it 'cannot be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part unless approved in writing'. The change states the agreement can be modified orally. This is a direct contradiction within section 9
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and can be modified orally, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part if both parties verbally agree on the change and document it via email.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement 'cannot be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part unless approved in writing.' The changed text states the agreement can be modified orally. The contradicted text also states the agreement can be modified orally. This is a direct contradiction according to the explanation given which highlights that the change introduces the possibility of oral modifications while the original agreement required written approval, which results in a contradiction.
